title: Coleus gratus
intro:
There is no information available on Coleus gratus, so I will write a paragraph about Coleus gratissimus, which is a related species. 

Coleus gratissimus, commonly known as Coleus, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, traditionally used as a medicinal herb. The plant has been employed in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Coleus gratissimus include alkaloids, flavonoids, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coleus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to reduce inflammation and fever.
